title: [phase:plan] [family:fallback-policy] [provider_hint:codex] Refine the fallback rule proposal for when Claude or GLM is unavailable in /vote and kernel orchestration. Return a short policy with allowed modes, forbidden substitutions, and migration steps. Be critical.
summary_text: The short policy should be stricter than the current router behavior. Right now the runtime requires the baseline trio for non-trivial writes, but the router still silently swaps `Codex -> GLM` and `GLM -> Codex` on lane failure, which weakens the meaning of “baseline trio success” and should be treated as a policy bug, not a feature: [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md#L91), [AGENTS.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/AGENTS.md#L107), [.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-router.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-router.yml#L899), [.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-router.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-router.yml#L981), [docs/kernel-sovereign-adapter-contract.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/docs/kernel-sovereign-adapter-contract.md#L72), [docs/requirements-gpt54-codex-kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/docs/requirements-gpt54-codex-kernel.md#L336). **Policy** - `normal`: `Codex + Claude + GLM` all available. `/vote` and Kernel may allow autonomous non-trivial writes under existing weighted `2/3` and HIGH-risk veto. - `degraded-review`: exactly one of `Claude` or `GLM` is unavailable. Planning, simulation, critique, and read-only work may continue, but `ok_to_execute=false` for non-trivial writes. - `shadow-substitute`: a substitute lane may run for observability only, but it must be marked `shadow`, excluded from baseline-family accounting, and excluded from auto-execution gates. - `explicit-bridge`: if Kernel must fall back operationally, use typed `fugue-bridge` handoff only. In legacy FUGUE, restrict automatic fallback to `record-only` or `backup-safe`; heavy execution stays manual-only. - `manual-emergency`: a human may override for incident handling, but only with explicit approval, visible trace/comment, and a rollback note. **Forbidden substitutions** - Do not count `GLM` as satisfied because a failed Claude lane was replaced by GLM. - Do not count `Claude` or `GLM` as satisfied because a failed GLM lane was replaced by Codex. - Do not promote `Gemini`, `xAI`, `copilot`, or other specialists into baseline-council status. - Do not collapse non-trivial autonomous writes to a 2-family council just to preserve lane count. - Do not encode Claude-specific outage logic in Kernel core; provider degradation stays inside the adapter, and Kernel fallback stays explicit and typed. **Migration steps** 1. Add explicit degraded states: `normal`, `missing-claude`, `missing-glm`, `missing-both`, and separate `baseline_family_success` from raw lane count. 2. Change cross-family fallbacks in `/vote` and local orchestration from voting replacements to `shadow` lanes that cannot satisfy trio gates. 3. Emit the same `fallback_packet` fields in both FUGUE and Kernel: missing family, reason, shadow lanes used, bridge used, human override present. 4. Keep Kernel default `codex-sovereign`; route any rollback through `fugue-bridge`, never hidden branching. 5. Add simulation tests for `Claude down`, `GLM down`, and `Claude+GLM down` asserting: read-only continuity allowed, non-trivial auto-write blocked, bridge/manual paths auditable. If you want, I can turn this into a repo-ready spec diff against `AGENTS.md` plus the Kernel adapter contract.
